target.title: cli-backend agent runs against anthropic (claude-cli runtime) return 401 authentication_failed while identical claude invocation works in shell
target.body:
# `cli-backend` agent runs against `anthropic` (claude-cli runtime) return `401 authentication_failed` while identical `claude` invocation works in shell

## Environment

- OpenClaw `2026.6.9 (c645ec4)` on macOS 25.3.0 (Apple silicon)
- Node `v22.22.2`
- Claude Code CLI `2.1.138` at `/opt/homebrew/bin/claude`
- Single macOS user (`claw`) running an openclaw-gateway LaunchAgent
- Auth state stripped to subscription-only profiles (no API keys anywhere):
  ```
  - anthropic effective=profiles | profiles=1 (oauth=1, token=0, api_key=0) | anthropic:claude-cli=OAuth
  - claude-cli  effective=profiles | profiles=1 (oauth=1) | anthropic:claude-cli=OAuth | synthetic=plugin-owned | source=Claude CLI native auth
  - openai      effective=profiles | profiles=1 (oauth=1) | openai:chatgpt-default=OAuth (ChatGPT subscription)
  ```
- Direct `claude -p "reply ok"` returns `ok` and successfully refreshes `~/.claude/.credentials.json` on demand.
- `openclaw infer model run --model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 --prompt 'ok'` returns `ok`. So the embedded provider path works fine against the same OAuth token.

## Symptom

Any agent run that is driven by the `cli-backend` path (cron jobs, Telegram-triggered agents using the claude-cli runtime) fails immediately with:

```
FailoverError: Failed to authenticate. API Error: 401 Invalid authentication credentials
```

Gateway log excerpts:

```
[agent/cli-backend] cli exec: provider=claude-cli model=claude-sonnet-4-6 promptChars=580 trigger=cron useResume=false session=none resumeSession=none reuse=none historyPrompt=none
[agent/cli-backend] claude live session start: provider=claude-cli model=claude-sonnet-4-6 activeSessions=1
[agent/cli-backend] claude live session close: provider=claude-cli model=claude-sonnet-4-6 reason=restart
[agent/cli-backend] claude live session turn failed: provider=claude-cli model=claude-sonnet-4-6 durationMs=4558 error=FailoverError
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 candidate=anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 reason=auth next=none detail=Failed to authenticate. API Error: 401 Invalid authentication credentials
```

## What I traced

I inserted a shell wrapper at `/Users/claw/.local/bin/claude` (earlier in PATH than `/opt/homebrew/bin/claude`) that logs argv + env + stdin and then `exec`s the real claude binary, capturing stdout/stderr.

**argv that openclaw passes to claude (from the wrapper log):**

```
-p
--include-partial-messages
--verbose
--setting-sources user
--allowedTools mcp__openclaw__*
--disallowedTools ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate
--strict-mcp-config
--mcp-config /var/folders/.../openclaw-cli-mcp-XXX/mcp.json
--plugin-dir /tmp/openclaw/openclaw-claude-skills-XXX/openclaw-skills
--model claude-sonnet-4-6
--append-system-prompt-file /private/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-cli-system-prompt-XXX/system-prompt.md
--input-format stream-json
--output-format stream-json
--permission-prompt-tool stdio
--replay-user-messages
--permission-mode default
```

**env passed to the claude subprocess** (auth-relevant subset):

```
HOME=/Users/claw
OPENCLAW_MCP_TOKEN=<token>
PATH=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/node@22/22.22.2_1/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/opt/homebrew/opt/node@22/bin:/Users/claw/.local/bin:/Users/claw/.npm-global/bin:...
```

Note: **no `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, no `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`, no `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`**.

**claude's stream output:**

```json
{"type":"system","subtype":"init","cwd":"/Users/claw/.openclaw/agents-staging/ai-news","session_id":"9e0a5024-...","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","permissionMode":"default","apiKeySource":"none","claude_code_version":"2.1.138",...}
{"type":"system","subtype":"status","status":"requesting","session_id":"9e0a5024-..."}
{"type":"system","subtype":"api_retry","attempt":1,"max_retries":10,"retry_delay_ms":558.94,"error_status":401,"error":"authentication_failed","session_id":"9e0a5024-..."}
{"type":"system","subtype":"api_retry","attempt":2,"max_retries":10,"retry_delay_ms":1089.89,"error_status":401,"error":"authentication_failed","session_id":"9e0a5024-..."}
{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":true,"api_error_status":401,"duration_ms":2199,"result":"Failed to authenticate. API Error: 401 Invalid authentication credentials","stop_reason":"stop_sequence",...}
```

Notice `apiKeySource:"none"` — claude is correctly using the OAuth token from `~/.claude/.credentials.json`, not an API key. Yet the API call returns 401.

## What's different about this invocation

Running the **exact same** argv, in the **exact same** cwd, with the **exact same** stdin, from my own interactive shell as the same `claw` user, returns `"ok"` with `cost=$0.025`. Verified by:

```sh
cd /Users/claw/.openclaw/agents-staging/ai-news
printf '{"type":"user","session_id":"","parent_tool_use_id":null,"message":{"role":"user","content":"reply ok"}}\n' | \
  /opt/homebrew/bin/claude -p --include-partial-messages --verbose --setting-sources user \
    --allowedTools 'mcp__openclaw__*' --disallowedTools ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate \
    --strict-mcp-config --mcp-config /tmp/repro/mcp.json --plugin-dir /tmp/repro/skills \
    --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --append-system-prompt-file /tmp/repro/system-prompt.md \
    --input-format stream-json --output-format stream-json \
    --permission-prompt-tool stdio --replay-user-messages --permission-mode default
# returns "ok"
```

The only meaningful differences I can identify:

1. The cron's `--mcp-config` points at a live openclaw HTTP MCP server (`http://127.0.0.1:61563/mcp`) with bearer-token auth via `OPENCLAW_MCP_TOKEN`. My repro uses `{"mcpServers": {}}`. claude's `init` payload confirms it connected to the openclaw MCP successfully (`mcp_servers: [{"name":"openclaw","status":"connected"}]`), so MCP isn't itself broken — but I wonder whether the cli-backend is mutating something on the OAuth credential file or sqlite between spawn and first request.

2. The cron's stdin includes `"session_id":""` (empty string) in the inbound user message. My passing repro uses the same and still gets `"ok"`.

3. The cron is the child of `openclaw-gateway`; my repro is the child of an interactive zsh.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Install openclaw 2026.6.9, sign in via `claude /login` (or transplant `~/.claude/.credentials.json`), then `openclaw models auth login --provider anthropic --method cli --set-default`.
2. Create an agent with `model.primary` = `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` (or any anthropic model). Add a one-shot cron, `--model anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`.
3. Trigger: `openclaw cron run <id>`.
4. Result: `FailoverError: Failed to authenticate. API Error: 401 Invalid authentication credentials`.
5. Manually re-run the exact argv claude was invoked with (visible in the gateway log or via a wrapper). It will return successfully.

## Workaround in production

Setting cron payload model to `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` (routed through `openai:chatgpt-default` OAuth, ChatGPT subscription) works end-to-end. So I've worked around by routing all cron-driven agents through the ChatGPT subscription path rather than the Claude subscription path.

But that's not really right — the user explicitly wants their Claude subscription used. Direct `claude -p` works. `openclaw infer` works. The cli-backend path is the only one that breaks.

## Hunches worth checking in the codebase

- `dist/external-cli-auth-selection-BDjB2d7f.js`: per-agent / per-profile selection logic around `CLAUDE_CLI_PROVIDER_ID = "claude-cli"` and the synthetic `anthropic:claude-cli` profile.
- `dist/claude-live-session-BFQhL7uS.js`: the live-session spawn path.
- `dist/credentials-read-D6J0ubHD.js`: where openclaw reads tokens. If this writes anything to credentials.json before spawning claude (e.g. to sync sqlite ↔ file), a race could invalidate the just-refreshed access token.

If a maintainer wants additional traces (full wrapper log, full system-prompt.md, full mcp.json, full claude stream output) I'm happy to attach them — I have all of them captured locally.

Thanks for openclaw!
target.author: cstreeter
